title: Threat Modeling, Privacy Engineering, and Abuse-Resistant Design · Decision Evidence Lens: Abuse Safety Case
summary: Security and privacy are represented by authentication, encryption, a scanner, and a policy page without system-specific adversarial evidence. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone abuse safety case increment in threat, privacy, and abuse assurance case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test misuse, automation, fraud, harassment, coercion, unsafe advice, resource exhaustion, evasion, and moderator or operator burden.
Analyze data action, affected people, likelihood, problematic consequence, controls, tradeoffs, and governance across the lifecycle.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Abuse Safety Case · Theory Abuse Safety Case
steps.instruction:
theory case — Authentication, encryption, a scanner, and a policy page substitute for a system-specific threat model, privacy risk assessment, abuse case, and response plan. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Test misuse, automation, fraud, harassment, coercion, unsafe advice, resource exhaustion, evasion, and moderator or operator burden.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Abuse Safety Case contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Abuse Safety Case · Theory Abuse Safety Case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test misuse, automation, fraud, harassment, coercion, unsafe advice, resource exhaustion, evasion, and moderator or operator burden. For Predict Abuse Safety Case · Theory Abuse Safety Case, keep Abuse Safety Case connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Abuse Safety Case condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GitHub healthy contribution and repository security documentation — Controls contribution guidance, governance, licensing, support, security policy, dependency review, code scanning, secret scanning, and repository stewardship.

options.text: Blocking known malicious strings prevents product abuse. That claim does not survive the predict abuse safety case evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Test misuse, automation, fraud, harassment, coercion, unsafe advice, resource exhaustion, evasion, and moderator or operator burden. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Authentication, encryption, a scanner, and a policy page substitute for a system-specific threat model, privacy risk assessment, abuse case, and response plan. The worked example variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Test misuse, automation, fraud, harassment, coercion, unsafe advice, resource exhaustion, evasion, and moderator or operator burden. A defensible solution applies Abuse Safety Case, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to threat, privacy, and abuse assurance case.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-2
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
